templates.summary_text: Your hearing test results today are within normal limits. No significant change has been detected. Continue to wear your hearing protection consistently in all noisy work areas.
templates.tech_notes: Normal result — no action required. Remind worker to continue wearing HPD. Retest within 24 months per SK OHS Regulations 1996.

templates.summary_text: Your hearing test today shows a significant finding at [freq] Hz in your [ear] ear. This result meets the criteria for an Abnormal finding under Saskatchewan OHS Regulations 1996. You are advised to see a physician or audiologist for further evaluation. Please ensure you are wearing appropriate, properly fitted hearing protection at all times in noisy work areas.
templates.tech_notes:
Abnormal — referral to physician or audiologist is required under SK OHS Regulations 1996. Written notification to worker required. Document HPD type, fit, and compliance.

Rule 201 triggered: Baseline A — average threshold >= 25 dB HL across 500–6000 Hz in either ear.
Rule 202 triggered: Periodic A — drop >= 15 dB at two adjacent frequencies (500–6000 Hz) vs baseline.
Rule 203 triggered: Periodic A — drop >= 25 dB at a single frequency (500–6000 Hz) vs baseline.

Edit the counsel text above to reflect which finding applies.

templates.summary_text: Your hearing test today shows an early warning finding of [shift] dB at [freq] Hz in your [ear] ear compared to your baseline. A follow-up hearing test is required within 24 months per Saskatchewan OHS Regulations 1996. It is important that you wear your hearing protection correctly and consistently in all noisy work areas.
templates.tech_notes: Periodic EW — shift of [shift] dB at [freq] Hz ([ear] ear) from baseline. Follow-up test required within 24 months per SK OHS Regulations 1996, s.113. Discuss HPD fit, type, and consistent use. Document any tinnitus complaints.
